nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 非法猎捕
nodes.summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。

nodes.name: 雁形目
nodes.summary: 雁形目（Anseriformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联166种鸟类，如中华秋沙鸭、角叫鴨、黑颈冠叫鸭等166种鸟类。

nodes.name: 动物界
nodes.summary: 动物界（Animalia）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 鸟纲
nodes.summary: 鸟纲（Aves）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门
nodes.summary: 脊索动物门（Chordata）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联10767种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭等10767种鸟类。

nodes.name: 斑胁草雁
nodes.summary: 斑胁草雁（Chloephaga picta）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如斑胁草雁。

nodes.name: 草雁屬
nodes.summary: 草雁屬（Chloephaga）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联5种鸟类，如黑翅草雁、斑胁草雁、白草雁等5种鸟类。

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 斑胁草雁
nodes.summary: 斑胁草雁（Upland goose，学名 Chloephaga picta），分类上属于雁形目，并归入鸭科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，常见栖息地包括稀树草原，主要威胁包括猎捕压力、非法猎捕。
nodes.shortSummary: 斑胁草雁（学名：Chloephaga picta），是雁形目鸭科的一种鸟类。 特征 斑胁草雁体重约2880.37克，翼长约412.2毫米，嘴峰长约40.1毫米，喙宽度约18毫米，喙厚度约14.7毫米，跗蹠长约72.5毫米，尾长约138.2毫米。斑胁草雁是部分迁徙的鸟类，其栖息在…

nodes.name: 鸭科
nodes.summary: 鸭科（Anatidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联162种鸟类，如中华秋沙鸭、白背鸭、细斑树鸭等162种鸟类。

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: Upland geese usually live in small, scattered groups on most pasture types (temperate grasslands, arid lowland scrubs), favouring areas with short green grasses

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: In the early 20th century, the Argentinian government declared the three Patagonian sheldgeese species (Ruddy-headed goose, Ashy-headed goose, and Upland goose) as pests due to claims of excessive grazing and negative effects on crop yields. Since then, wildlife agencies have encouraged hunting of upland geese across the entirety of their range and without restrictions on the number of birds killed

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Consequently, population surveys started suggesting continental populations were declining. In 2008, all three species were classified as endangered by the Argentine government and hunting was banned, although some poaching still occurs, mostly on the mainland. Recently, fox predation has drastically reduced population numbers on the mainland, so national governments have established several protected areas throughout Patagonia in an attempt to conserve the species
